reasoning: The flagged 'unsafe' behavior in src/postinst only affects Debian systems, not Arch. The script isn't executed during Arch package installation and poses no risk.
disagreement_analysis: qwen misclassified src/postinst as unsafe by ignoring Arch-specific context where the script isn't executed.
false_positives: src/postinst (qwen): Misinterpreted Debian-specific APT setup as active threat on Arch where script isn't executed
learnings: Improve context awareness: Models should better recognize distribution-specific execution contexts (e.g., Debian scripts in AUR packages)
Prioritize execution pathways: Focus audits on files actually executed during makepkg/pacman operations
Enhance false-positive detection: Flag scripts only when they're invoked in target environment
